title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Currying is a technique of transforming a function that takes multiple arguments into a sequence of functions that each take one argument.

f(a, b, c) → f(a)(b)(c)

Why it's useful:
• Partial application — pre-fill some arguments to create specialised functions.
• Function composition — chain small, single-argument functions.
• Reusability — create configurable utility functions.

Currying doesn't call the function; it transforms how it is called. Partial application calls the function with some arguments fixed.
